front: Play: Sir Oliver Surface visits his nephews Joseph and Charles in disguise, posing as 'Mr. Stanley' and as a moneylender with Moses's help
back: The School for Scandal (Richard Brinsley Sheridan)

front: Play: the famous 'screen scene' — a folding screen is pulled down to reveal Lady Teazle's affair with Joseph Surface
back: The School for Scandal (Richard Brinsley Sheridan)

front: Play: Charles sells family portraits at auction but refuses to sell his uncle Oliver's picture
back: The School for Scandal (Richard Brinsley Sheridan)

front: Play: Mrs. Malaprop calls someone 'the very pineapple of politeness' and describes 'headstrong as an allegory on the banks of the Nile'
Play: Jack Absolute pretends to be 'Ensign Beverley' to woo Lydia Languish
Play: Sir Lucius O'Trigger almost duels Jack Absolute, having sent love letters to Mrs. Malaprop thinking she was 'Delia'
Play: Mr. Sneer and Mr. Dangle attend a rehearsal of Mr. Puff's The Spanish Armada
